sentences.text: The choir performed a cappella in the cathedral.
She prefers to sing a cappella without any instruments.
Their final song was sung a cappella and moved the audience deeply.
The rescue team had to abseil down the cliff.
She learned to abseil safely during the outdoor course.
Tourists watched firefighters abseil from the roof.
The two sides reached an accord after weeks of talks.
His story does not accord with the evidence.
The policy is in accord with international law.
Her admonitory tone warned us not to repeat the mistake.
The sign carried an admonitory message about fire safety.
He gave the class an admonitory look before the test began.
Some artists tried to africanize imported styles with local themes.
The movement aimed to africanize the school curriculum.
Scholars debated whether the institution should africanize its policies.
They served the fish grilled, Mediterranean style, a la carte.
The designer created a Parisian look, ala the 1920s.
He ordered dinner ala carte instead of choosing a set menu.
We ate alfresco under the garden lights.
The hotel offers alfresco dining by the sea.
They moved the party alfresco when the weather improved.
He seemed aloof at first, but he was actually shy.
Her aloof manner made teamwork difficult.
The actor stayed aloof from the media after the scandal.
The ambiguity of the contract caused a legal dispute.
Writers sometimes use ambiguity on purpose.
There was no room for ambiguity in the instructions.
His reply was too ambiguous to be useful.
The law remains ambiguous in this area.
She gave an ambiguous smile and changed the subject.
The soldiers were caught in an ambush at dawn.
The reporter tried to ambush the minister with questions.
Hunters once used the forest path for an ambush.
He suffered an anaphylactic reaction after eating peanuts.
Doctors treated the patient for an anaphylactic shock.
People with severe allergies fear anaphylactic episodes.
The audience waited in anticipation for the curtain to rise.
She packed in anticipation of a long delay.
The game was released to great anticipation worldwide.
The apiarist checked the hives at sunrise.
An experienced apiarist taught us how to handle bees safely.
The village apiarist sells honey at the weekend market.
The film imagines life after an apocalypse.
Some headlines described the storm as an apocalypse.
The novel combines faith, fear, and apocalypse.
He was apoplectic when he saw the damage to his car.
The coach looked apoplectic after the final goal.
Her apoplectic reaction surprised everyone in the office.
Koalas are arboreal animals that spend most of their lives in trees.
The reserve protects several rare arboreal species.
Scientists studied the arboreal habits of the monkeys.
The manual was full of arcane technical terms.
Only a few experts understood the arcane system.
He enjoys reading about arcane rituals and symbols.
Witnesses can attest to her honesty.
These records attest to the age of the building.
Many teachers attest that the new method works.
The school auditorium was full for the concert.
Please meet me outside the main auditorium.
The speaker walked onto the stage in the dark auditorium.
The novel describes life under an authoritarian regime.
His parenting style is too authoritarian for modern families.
Students resisted the school's authoritarian rules.
The doctor ordered an autopsy to confirm the cause of death.
The report included the results of the autopsy.
Police waited for the autopsy before making a statement.
Quick action helped avert a disaster.
She averted her eyes when the lights came on.
Leaders met to avert a wider conflict.
The waterfall was an awesome sight in the morning sun.
He did an awesome job on the design project.
The mountain range looked awesome from the plane.
The speech was full of banal phrases.
Critics dismissed the film as banal and predictable.
He turned a banal topic into an interesting essay.
Many brands jumped on the eco-friendly bandwagon.
She refused to join the bandwagon just to fit in.
Investors often rush onto the bandwagon too late.
The village square was shaded by a huge banyan tree.
Children played beneath the roots of the banyan.
The banyan spread across almost the entire garden.
His joke contained a barb aimed at the manager.
The hook had a sharp barb that caught in the net.
She ignored the little barb hidden in his compliment.
A heavy barge moved slowly along the river.
You cannot just barge into my office without knocking.
The workers loaded sand onto the barge.
The gardener protected each bazseedling from heavy rain.
A single bazseedling pushed through the dark soil.
They moved the fragile bazseedling into a warmer tray.
We watched the beekeeper open the beehive carefully.
The old tree contained a wild beehive.
Honey was dripping from the damaged beehive.
His calm voice belied his fear.
The cheerful colors belie the sadness of the story.
Her smile belied the pain she was feeling.
The doctor confirmed that the tumor was benign.
At first, the weather looked benign and pleasant.
He gave me a benign smile before leaving.
She took bereavement leave after her father's death.
Counseling can help people deal with bereavement.
The group supports families through grief and bereavement.
The crowd went berserk after the winning goal.
He was afraid the machine would go berserk again.
The tabloids claimed she went berserk in the restaurant.
The brothers still bicker about money.
Small teams often bicker under pressure.
They continued to bicker through the entire drive.
The article was filled with bile and anger.
Doctors examined the patient's bile levels.
He spoke with obvious bile about his former partners.
Smoke began to billow from the warehouse roof.
Her dress billowed in the sea breeze.
Dark clouds billowed across the horizon.
Music continued to blare from the next apartment.
The alarm began to blare at midnight.
Car horns blared in the traffic jam.
The future looked bleak after the factory closed.
They crossed a bleak stretch of empty land.
His report offered a bleak view of the economy.
He seemed like a friendly bloke from the neighborhood.
That bloke by the door asked for you earlier.
The old bloke at the shop remembers everyone by name.
The company made a boatload of money from the deal.
We carried in a boatload of supplies before the storm.
He bought a boatload of books at the market.
She made a bodacious entrance in a bright red coat.
The chef created a bodacious dessert with impossible amounts of cream.
It was a bodacious plan, but somehow it worked.
The critics called them botchers after the failed repair job.
Only botchers would leave wiring exposed like that.
The article mocked the botchers who ruined the restoration.

ch: 拙劣的工匠／把事情搞砸的人

sentences.text: Bracken covered the hillside after the rain.
The path disappeared under thick bracken.
They pushed through wet bracken to reach the stream.
A fire brigade arrived within minutes.
The volunteer brigade helped clear the fallen trees.
A medical brigade was sent to the disaster zone.
He tends to bristle at even mild criticism.
The brush had stiff bristles made of wire.
She saw his beard bristle when he got angry.
The athlete won a bronze medal in the final.
They restored an old bronze statue in the square.
His skin turned bronze after a week in the sun.
The rabbit disappeared into its burrow.
Some animals burrow underground for safety.
The child liked to burrow under the blankets.
There was a buzz of excitement before the announcement.
My phone began to buzz during the lecture.
Bees buzzed around the flowers all afternoon.
The flood was a calamity for the farming town.
They feared the drought would become a national calamity.
His careless remark triggered a small social calamity.
Engineers calibrate the machine every morning.
You should calibrate the scale before using it.
The lab technician carefully calibrated each instrument.
Years of guitar practice left a callus on his finger.
The doctor said the thick skin was only a callus.
A painful callus formed on her heel.
The firm feared the new phone would cannibalize sales of the old model.
Mechanics cannibalized one engine to repair another.
The larger chain began to cannibalize its own market.
The novel is now part of the literary canon.
The museum questions who gets included in the artistic canon.
Fans argue about whether the film is canon.
The hikers rested under the forest canopy.
A glass canopy covered the entrance.
Birds nested high in the tropical canopy.
The oil spill was an environmental catastrophe.
Doctors acted fast to avert a catastrophe.
The book explores how one error became a catastrophe.
Ancient sailors navigated by celestial bodies.
The painting showed a celestial scene above the clouds.
They studied celestial motion in the observatory.
The hero rode into battle on a golden chariot.
The museum displayed an ancient ceremonial chariot.
The film's opening scene featured a racing chariot.
The defeat seemed to chasten the entire team.
Experience may chasten even the most arrogant leader.
He was chastened by the public criticism.
The ceiling was painted with tiny cherubs.
The baby looked like a little cherub in the photo.
A marble cherub stood beside the fountain.
She gently chided him for being late again.
Teachers should correct, not publicly chide, their students.
He was chided for wasting company resources.
The church bells began to chime at noon.
Your idea chimes with what I was thinking.
A soft chime announced the elevator's arrival.
Sunlight came through a chink in the wall.
They spotted a chink in the opponent's defense.
A tiny chink of light appeared under the door.
The old train began to chuff away from the station.
He sounded quite chuffed with his exam result.
Steam engines used to chuff through the valley.
He brought his old school chum to the reunion.
The fishermen used chum to attract larger fish.
She still meets her college chum every summer.
The sea began to churn before the storm hit.
The company churns out cheap products every month.
Her stomach churned with anxiety before the speech.
A loud clang came from the factory floor.
The gate shut with a heavy clang.
We heard metal pipes clang in the wind.
The tiny room made him feel claustrophobic.
I avoid crowded lifts because they feel claustrophobic.
The tunnel looked dark and claustrophobic.
Her final argument was the clincher in the debate.
The price reduction proved to be the clincher.
One decisive statistic was the clincher for the jury.
Hair can clog the bathroom drain.
Traffic quickly clogged the main road.
Wet leaves clogged the gutters after the storm.
No one should coerce staff into unpaid work.
The gang tried to coerce him into silence.
Threats were used to coerce witnesses.
Please collate the survey results before the meeting.
The editor collated several versions of the manuscript.
Researchers collated the data into one report.
A loud commotion broke out near the stage.
We heard a commotion outside and ran to the window.
The sudden arrival of reporters caused a commotion.
Guests receive a complimentary drink on arrival.
She was highly complimentary about the new exhibition.
The hotel offered complimentary breakfast to all visitors.
The charity acts as a conduit for donated funds.
Water flows through a concrete conduit below the road.
The app became a conduit for false information.
The scholar specialized in Confucian philosophy.
Some schools still reflect Confucian values.
The book compares Confucian and Western traditions.
A consortium of firms funded the new project.
The university joined an international research consortium.
The airport deal was signed by a banking consortium.
She wore a conspicuous hat at the ceremony.
His absence was conspicuous during the meeting.
There was a conspicuous lack of detail in the report.
The witness gave contradictory statements.
Their goals seem contradictory but can be balanced.
The article sounded contradictory from start to finish.
Rising sea temperatures threaten coral reefs.
The diver photographed bright coral underwater.
Broken coral washed up on the beach after the storm.
The essay explored a counterfactual version of history.
Counterfactual thinking can help people reflect on decisions.
The novelist built an entire counterfactual world around one change.
The government announced a crackdown on illegal dumping.
A police crackdown followed the rise in street crime.
Activists feared a crackdown on public protests.
Students should not cram the night before an exam.
They tried to cram too many topics into one lesson.
We had to cram into the last train home.
A crane lifted the steel beam into place.
He craned his neck to see over the crowd.
A white crane stood motionless by the lake.
The old radio was powered by a hand crank.
He sounded like a crank, but some of his points were valid.
Turn the crank slowly to raise the flag.
After the hike, we all craved cold water.
Some people crave attention more than success.
She craved a quiet life far from the city.
At first, few people gave credence to the rumor.
The evidence lends credence to her account.
No serious historian gives credence to that myth.
He listened credulously to the scammer's story.
The child looked up credulously and asked another question.
Voters should not accept such promises credulously.
The sunset turned the sky crimson.
She wore a crimson scarf in winter.
The leaves became crimson in late autumn.
The autumn air felt cold and crisp.
The waiter served crisp salad with the meal.
Her answer was crisp and confident.
Some strange little critter had eaten the tomatoes.
The kids found a furry critter under the deck.
Every critter in the forest seemed active at dusk.
War became a crucible that shaped his character.
The metal was heated in a crucible.
The city was a crucible of political change.
The old wall began to crumble after years of rain.
She watched the cookie crumble in her hand.
His confidence started to crumble under pressure.
The exhibition was the culmination of five years of work.
Their victory marked the culmination of a long campaign.
The book feels like the culmination of his ideas.
Customers can pick up orders at the curbside counter.
The driver stopped at the curbside for a moment.
Curbside collection reduced waiting times in the city.
It would be daft to ignore such clear evidence.
He made a daft joke at the wrong moment.
Buying it twice would be completely daft.
The bright lights can dazzle drivers at night.
She managed to dazzle the judges with her final performance.
The jewelry shop was designed to dazzle passers-by.
A well-known debunker exposed the viral hoax.
The book made him popular as a myth debunker.
Online debunkers quickly challenged the false claim.
The case involved years of deliberate deception.
She was hurt by the deception more than the loss.
Magic shows rely on skillful deception.

ch: 埃塞俄比亞的／埃塞俄比亞人
一群鵝／嘰嘰喳喳的一群人
不合群的人／不合適的東西
意外發現美好事物的運氣
不屈不撓的／持續不減的
